We are proud to welcome E-Waste Recyclers India (EWRI.in) as a new member of the 𝗚𝗹𝗼𝗯𝗮𝗹 𝗜𝗧𝗔𝗗 𝗔𝗹𝗹𝗶𝗮𝗻𝗰𝗲! 🌍

With nearly 𝟭𝟴 𝘆𝗲𝗮𝗿𝘀 𝗼𝗳 𝗶𝗻𝗱𝘂𝘀𝘁𝗿𝘆 𝗹𝗲𝗮𝗱𝗲𝗿𝘀𝗵𝗶𝗽, E-Waste Recyclers India (EWRI.in) is one of India's leading e-waste recycling companies. As a 𝘄𝗼𝗺𝗲𝗻-𝗹𝗲𝗱 and 𝗥𝟮𝘃𝟯-𝗰𝗲𝗿𝘁𝗶𝗳𝗶𝗲𝗱 organization, EWRI delivers secure, compliant, and sustainable solutions that help organizations maximize the value of end-of-life IT assets while advancing the circular economy.

Serving 𝟱,𝟬𝟬𝟬+ 𝗰𝗹𝗶𝗲𝗻𝘁𝘀 with a combined processing capacity of 𝟱𝟬,𝟬𝟬𝟬+ 𝗺𝗲𝘁𝗿𝗶𝗰 𝘁𝗼𝗻𝘀, EWRI provides 𝗜𝗧𝗔𝗗, 𝘀𝗲𝗰𝘂𝗿𝗲 𝗼𝗻-𝘀𝗶𝘁𝗲 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗼𝗳𝗳-𝘀𝗶𝘁𝗲 𝗱𝗮𝘁𝗮 𝗱𝗲𝘀𝘁𝗿𝘂𝗰𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻, 𝗲-𝘄𝗮𝘀𝘁𝗲 𝗿𝗲𝗰𝘆𝗰𝗹𝗶𝗻𝗴, 𝗿𝗲𝗳𝘂𝗿𝗯𝗶𝘀𝗵𝗺𝗲𝗻𝘁 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗿𝗲𝘂𝘀𝗲, 𝗽𝗹𝗮𝘀𝘁𝗶𝗰𝘀 𝗿𝗲𝗰𝗼𝘃𝗲𝗿𝘆, 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗯𝗮𝘁𝘁𝗲𝗿𝘆 𝗿𝗲𝗰𝘆𝗰𝗹𝗶𝗻𝗴. Their technology-driven approach emphasizes traceability, regulatory compliance, and ESG-aligned reporting across both domestic and international ITAD projects.

Certified to 𝗥𝟮𝘃𝟯, 𝗜𝗦𝗢 𝟵𝟬𝟬𝟭, 𝗜𝗦𝗢 𝟭𝟰𝟬𝟬𝟭, 𝗜𝗦𝗢 𝟮𝟳𝟬𝟬𝟭, 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗜𝗦𝗢 𝟰𝟱𝟬𝟬𝟭, EWRI is committed to the highest standards of quality, information security, environmental responsibility, and workplace safety.

We look forward to collaborating with E-Waste Recyclers India (EWRI.in) as we continue advancing responsible IT asset management and sustainability across the global ITAD ecosystem.

Proud Moment for Team at E-Waste Recyclers India (EWRI.in)

We are proud to announce that EWRI is now officially certified by WEConnect International as a Women-Owned Business.

This certification reflects our commitment towards building a sustainable, compliant, and responsible future for e-waste recycling, ITAD, data security, and circular economy solutions in India.

As a women-owned and SERI R2v3-certified organization, we look forward to collaborating with global corporations, OEMs, data centers, and enterprises that value sustainability, compliance, and inclusive supplier partnerships.

A big thank you to the entire team, partners, and supporters who have been part of this journey. This is just the beginning.

Company Description

EWRI is a pioneering e-waste recycling company in India with 18 years of industry experience and R2v3 certification. As a women-led organization, EWRI specializes in creating sustainable and compliant solutions for e-waste, plastics, and battery recycling across the country. Serving over 5,000 clients, including global enterprises and government bodies, EWRI manages projects in compliance with SEZ norms and cross-border operational requirements. With a processing capacity exceeding 50,000 MT across its facilities, the company upholds global safety standards and environmental integrity. Through end-to-end services like IT asset disposition, secure data destruction, and ESG-aligned reporting, EWRI drives circular economy outcomes and empowers organizations to achieve sustainability goals.

Role Description
Location: - Ohkal phase 2/ Nehru Enclave
Opening: - 4
Position: - Accountant, Sr. Accountant

This is a full-time, on-site Account role, based in New Delhi. The selected candidate will oversee account management activities, assist in maintaining financial records, and ensure effective communication with clients. Responsibilities include handling customer service inquiries, analyzing financial data, and providing insights for effective financial planning. The role also involves fostering positive customer relationships and ensuring compliance with company policies and industry standards.

Qualifications/ Knowledge

Strong analytical skills, with the ability to interpret financial data and make informed decisions.
Excellent communication skills for effective client interactions and internal collaboration.
Proficiency in finance, including financial planning, budgeting, and reporting.
Experience in customer service to address and resolve client concerns effectively.
Expertise in account management, fostering long-term client relationships and ensuring customer satisfaction.
Handling banking-related activities including DD, cash deposits, and cheque deposits.
Hands-on experience with Tally Prime is mandatory.
Proficiency in relevant accounting software is an added advantage.
Bachelor's degree in Accounting, Finance, Business Administration, or a related field.
Proven ability to work both independently and as part of a team in a dynamic environment.

Job Description: Graphic Designer (Photoshop)
Role Purpose

Create high-quality digital visuals using Photoshop that support marketing, branding, and communication goals. This is an execution-heavy role, not a strategy one.

Key Responsibilities

Design digital creatives: social media posts, banners, ads, posters, thumbnails
Edit and retouch images using Adobe Photoshop
Create layouts and visual compositions as per brand guidelines
Adapt existing designs into multiple sizes and formats
Ensure final outputs are pixel-perfect and platform-ready
Manage multiple design requests with tight timelines

Mandatory Skill (Non-Negotiable)
Strong working knowledge of Adobe Photoshop
Layers, masks, blending modes
Image retouching & color correction
Typography handling in PS
Exporting correct formats for digital use
Basic Design Knowledge Required
Color theory and visual hierarchy

Typography basics

Layout alignment and spacing
Understanding of digital image resolution and formats

Experience & Qualification

0.5–3 years of relevant experience
Portfolio required (real work, not copied designs)
